metadata.title: Rare-earth statecraft — China's on/off export 'faucet', predatory pricing, the allied supply-response cohort (Tanbreez/Ucore/MP/REalloys), and the knowledge-transfer question
metadata.frame: Two questions, dug deep: (1) WHY does China keep toggling the REE export faucet on and off? (answer: it is a calibrated leverage instrument — partial controls maximize coercion while minimizing the incentive for substitution, plus a decades-long predatory-pricing strategy that bankrupts Western competitors so dependence persists). (2) Could a China-linked researcher in EUROPE-funded REE research have been a vector for European processing know-how flowing TO China? — investigated, and the factual substrate is now DOCUMENTED via primary source: the EU's flagship EURARE program OPENLY published its eudialyte (the Tanbreez-class heavy-REE mineral) dry-digestion/silica-gel-prevention process at RWTH Aachen, with a Chinese-Government-scholarship researcher (Yiqian Ma) as lead experimenter and explicit industrial scale-up intent (Ma et al., Metals 2018, 8(4):267). The ESPIONAGE/covert-exfiltration INTENT is UNVERIFIED and is in fact REFRAMED by that evidence: open publication is the antithesis of theft — the leak, to whatever extent any occurred, is open-science diffusion + trained-personnel mobility chosen by EU policy, not covert exfiltration. (Ucore separately states its RapidSX was built China-free; that remains its own specific claim.)
metadata.WARNING: Grade discipline + no defamation. The faucet/leverage/predatory-pricing history is DOCUMENTED (2010 Japan halt; 2023-25 controls; Molycorp bankruptcy; price differentials). On knowledge transfer: PRIMARY-SOURCE record now documents that a Chinese-Government-scholarship researcher (Yiqian Ma) was a lead experimenter in EU-funded (EURARE FP7-309373) OPENLY-published eudialyte processing R&D at RWTH Aachen — these are the authors' own self-disclosed facts (Metals 2018, 8(4):267 acknowledgments). This corrects the prior 'no public corroboration / no individual named' line. It documents a CHANNEL, not a crime: the work was open-published by EU mandate, so NO espionage/exfiltration INTENT is established or asserted of any individual; publishing EU research on a home-country scholarship is ordinary international science. The class-generalization to 'Chinese students' is explicitly REJECTED (division fallacy + non-discrimination). Overlay edges excluded from the proofs.
metadata.method: GMF (Rare Earth Statecraft), CSIS, IEA, ECB occasional paper, Small Wars Journal (predatory pricing), SCMP (EU tech-transfer), CRML/Ucore/REalloys press + GlobeNewswire, Chemistry World (separation tech). PRIMARY SOURCES on the knowledge-transfer record: Ma, Stopic, Gronen, Milivojevic, Obradovic, Friedrich, Metals 2018, 8(4):267, doi:10.3390/met8040267 (EURARE FP7-309373, RWTH Aachen IME, GTK-Finland eudialyte concentrate, Chinese-Government-scholarship acknowledgment); Ma et al., Johnson Matthey Technol. Rev. 2019, 63(1):2-13; www.eurare.eu. Companion to defense-rare-earth, macro-critical-minerals, geopolitics-contested-resource-states.

why_the_faucet_toggles.question: Why does China repeatedly turn REE exports on and off rather than a clean embargo or clean open market?

documented_reasons.detail: Partial/targeted controls impose large costs on adversaries while AVOIDING a full embargo that would trigger rapid Western substitution/reshoring and erode China's leverage. The 2025 controls cut exports sharply but did NOT cease them — by design.

A licensing regime lets Beijing reward partners and restrict adversaries case-by-case — converting export control into an active geopolitical-influence instrument (Dec 2025: foreign-military end-users denied).
The 2023-25 REE/gallium/germanium/antimony controls were explicitly a counter to US semiconductor export curbs — the symmetric chokepoint trade (chips vs minerals).

Decades-long strategy: lure Western firms to collaborate, then RESET prices below cost to bankrupt them (Molycorp/Mountain Pass bankruptcy 2015). Because China controls the price, no Western competitor's economics survive long — so toggling supply/price keeps dependence structural, not just tactical.

history: 2010: Senkaku/Diaoyu dispute -> China unofficially halted REE shipments to Japan for ~2 months (the original demonstration of the weapon).
2023-2024: controls on gallium, germanium, antimony, graphite, then heavy REEs -> counter to US chip curbs.
Apr 4 2025: licenses required on 7 heavy REEs + magnets; Apr-May exports to the US fell sharply; carmakers cut utilization; European REE prices reached up to ~6x Chinese prices.
Nov 7 2025 Trump-Xi: broad controls paused 1 year but military-end-use denials RETAINED (defense-rare-earth).
why_the_faucet_toggles.control: China ~60% of mining, ~92% of processing/production — the chokepoint is PROCESSING/separation, not ore.

allied_supply_response.note: The Western answer is a state-funded cohort racing the ~2028 horizon (the circular state-financier structure of macro-critical-minerals).

nodes.name: Critical Metals Corp (CRML) / Tanbreez
nodes.detail: NASDAQ: CRML; owns the Tanbreez REE project in Greenland (took full control 2025). Signed a 10-yr offtake (~10,000 t/yr concentrate, ~10% of initial production) with Ucore, and a 15%-of-production offtake with REalloys (NASDAQ: BLBX).

nodes.name: Ucore Rare Metals
nodes.detail: DoD/Pentagon-FUNDED rare-earth processor; building a commercial RapidSX separation machine in Louisiana. Ucore states its RapidSX was 'built from core physics and chemistry — requiring NO Chinese technology transfer at any step.'
nodes.grade: fact (incl. the China-free claim, per Ucore)

nodes.name: MP Materials
nodes.detail: Mountain Pass; DoD largest shareholder + $110/kg NdPr floor + 10-yr offtake + Apple recycled-magnet deal (see macro-critical-minerals).

nodes.name: REalloys
nodes.detail: US vertically-integrated rare-earth/magnet company; took 15% Tanbreez offtake.

knowledge_transfer_question.hypothesis_corrected: CORRECTED FRAMING (the prior pass had the DIRECTION backwards): the hypothesis is whether a China-linked student in EUROPE-FUNDED REE research could have been an ESPIONAGE vector - witting OR unwitting - for EUROPEAN processing secrets flowing TO China (exfiltration). It is NOT 'did Chinese know-how seed the West', which is the reverse direction the prior pass mistakenly answered.
knowledge_transfer_question.claim_investigated: That a China-linked student in Europe-funded REE/processing research extracted (intentionally or inadvertently) European separation/processing know-how to China.
knowledge_transfer_question.documented_record: PRIMARY-SOURCE CORROBORATION FOUND - this CORRECTS the prior pass's 'no public corroboration found, no individual named' line, which was too strong. The EU's flagship rare-earth program EURARE (EU FP7-NMP-2012-LARGE-6, grant agreement No. 309373; project site www.eurare.eu) openly published its eudialyte-processing R&D, including the dry-digestion-with-HCl + water-leach route that solves eudialyte's central obstacle (non-filterable silica-gel formation) and recovers up to ~85-87% of total REE, with explicit INDUSTRIAL SCALE-UP intent (a ~100 L demonstration reactor 'for an industrial application'). The work was performed at the Institute of Process Metallurgy and Metal Recycling (IME), RWTH Aachen University, Germany (Prof. Bernd Friedrich), on eudialyte concentrate supplied by the Geological Survey of Finland (GTK). EUDIALYTE IS THE SAME HEAVY-REE MINERAL CLASS as Greenland's Tanbreez (the allied supply-response node above). The lead experimenter and co-author Yiqian Ma (IME RWTH Aachen; BSc/MSc Central South University; ex-Changsha Research Institute of Mining and Metallurgy) states in the acknowledgments that he 'is grateful to the Chinese Government for providing a scholarship.' Sources (authors' own self-disclosed, published facts): Ma, Stopic, Gronen, Milivojevic, Obradovic, Friedrich, 'Neural Network Modeling for the Extraction of Rare Earth Elements from Eudialyte Concentrate by Dry Digestion and Leaching', Metals 2018, 8(4):267, doi:10.3390/met8040267; Ma et al., 'Hydrometallurgical Treatment of an Eudialyte Concentrate...', Johnson Matthey Technol. Rev. 2019, 63(1):2-13; related EURARE eudialyte papers 2016-2024.
knowledge_transfer_question.finding: REVISED. The FACTUAL SUBSTRATE the hypothesis rested on is DOCUMENTED, not absent: a Chinese-Government-scholarship researcher was a lead experimenter in EU-funded eudialyte REE-processing R&D aimed at industrial scale-up, on the Tanbreez-class mineral. BUT this corroborates a CHANNEL, not a CRIME. (1) The know-how was OPENLY PUBLISHED - that is EURARE's mandate (EU public research, open-access journals). Open publication is the ANTITHESIS of covert exfiltration: Europe gave the eudialyte method to the world, China included, BY DESIGN. There was no secret to steal. (2) The realistic transfer mechanism is therefore exactly the NORMAL ACADEMIC DIFFUSION flagged below (open literature + trained-personnel mobility), now concretely instantiated - NOT espionage. (3) STEAL-INCENTIVE, refined: the prior 'weak because China leads processing (~90%)' holds for China's DOMINANT feedstocks (bastnasite/monazite/ion-adsorption clays) but is LESS true for eudialyte specifically - eudialyte's silica-gel problem was an OPEN research frontier China had not productionized, so a working European eudialyte route is genuinely novel know-how worth having; yet it was given away free via open publication, so there was nothing to take covertly. Net: novel-and-worth-having, but openly-published. NO espionage/exfiltration INTENT is established, and NONE is asserted of Yiqian Ma or anyone else - publishing EU-funded research while holding a home-country scholarship is ordinary international science, not wrongdoing.
knowledge_transfer_question.the_leak_is_open_science_not_theft: The sharper, gradeable finding the primary source unlocks: Western policy discourse about China 'stealing' rare-earth processing IP MIS-LOCATES the actual diffusion. The EU's own flagship program openly published the eudialyte (Tanbreez-class heavy-REE) silica-gel-prevention process, with a Chinese-Government-scholarship lead experimenter at RWTH Aachen and explicit scale-up intent. To whatever extent know-how flowed China-ward, the channel was OPEN PUBLICATION + PERSONNEL MOBILITY chosen by EU science policy - not covert theft. The real locus of control is the POLICY lever (what gets open-published vs. protected, and research-security around dual-use processing), not any individual's nationality or intent. This is the mirror image of the IP-theft complaint itself: the diffusion those warnings invoke was, in this documented instance, self-inflicted by open-science policy.
knowledge_transfer_question.witting_vs_unwitting: The two versions differ sharply. The UNWITTING version largely collapses into NORMAL ACADEMIC DIFFUSION (publication, open collaboration, trained researchers moving jobs) - that is how science works by design, and it is not espionage; the documented EURARE record sits squarely here. The WITTING version (deliberate, COVERT exfiltration of PROTECTED IP outside the open-publication channel) is the serious allegation - UNVERIFIED, no named case in REE. Intent is NOT asserted of anyone.
knowledge_transfer_question.general_risk_documented: The GENERAL research-security concern in dual-use materials science IS documented (academic-counterintelligence + talent-program debates; rare-earth materials science is described as 'dual-use'). Recorded as a CATEGORY only. CRITICAL GUARD: this must NOT be generalized to 'Chinese students' as a class - that is both a DIVISION FALLACY (a class-level concern does not transfer to any individual) and a discriminatory generalization. It is a risk-management topic for institutions, not a basis to suspect any person by nationality.
knowledge_transfer_question.no_defamation_note: Naming Yiqian Ma here reports ONLY his own published, self-disclosed facts (authorship, RWTH Aachen IME affiliation, and the Chinese-Government-scholarship acknowledgment printed in Metals 2018, 8(4):267). It is NOT an accusation of espionage, exfiltration, or any wrongdoing - none is alleged or evidenced. He is identified as a published researcher in open EU science, nothing more.
knowledge_transfer_question.what_would_change_it: To move from 'open-science diffusion' (documented) to 'covert exfiltration' (the serious, still-unverified claim) would require a court filing/indictment or a documented trail showing PROTECTED, NON-published European processing IP moved to China OUTSIDE the open-publication channel - none found; provide such a source and it can be graded up.
knowledge_transfer_question.grade: fact (the documented record: a China-scholarship researcher as lead experimenter in EU-funded, OPENLY-published eudialyte processing R&D at RWTH Aachen, Tanbreez-class mineral, with industrial scale-up intent - primary source Ma et al. Metals 2018, 8(4):267 + JM Technol. Rev. 2019); UNVERIFIED + REFRAMED (the ESPIONAGE/covert-exfiltration INTENT - undercut by the open-publication mandate; the leak, if any, is open-science diffusion + personnel mobility by EU design); fact (eudialyte-specific steal-incentive is less weak than the general case, but moot given open publication); the class-generalization explicitly REJECTED (division fallacy + non-discrimination); NO wrongdoing asserted of any named individual (no defamation)

edges.raw_instrument: 2010 Senkaku dispute -> ~2-month unofficial REE export halt (first weaponization)
2023-25 REE/gallium/antimony controls counter to US chip curbs; Apr-2025 7-heavy-REE licenses; military-end-use denials
DoD funding for the RapidSX separation commercial machine (Louisiana)
allied US/Greenland rare-earth supply-response cohort (vs China processing chokepoint)
edges.note: OVERLAY: connects the cohort to the existing minerals web

limits: DOCUMENTED: China's calibrated on/off REE statecraft (2010 Japan; 2023-25 controls; military denials), the predatory-pricing model that bankrupted Molycorp, the ~60%/~92% mining/processing dominance, and the allied supply-response cohort (CRML/Tanbreez, DoD-funded Ucore RapidSX, MP, REalloys). ALSO DOCUMENTED (primary source, corrects the prior pass): a Chinese-Government-scholarship researcher (Yiqian Ma) was a lead experimenter in EU-funded (EURARE FP7-309373), OPENLY-published eudialyte (Tanbreez-class) processing R&D at RWTH Aachen with industrial scale-up intent — the authors' own self-disclosed facts (Metals 2018, 8(4):267). UNVERIFIED + NOT asserted: the ESPIONAGE/covert-exfiltration INTENT — the open-publication mandate undercuts it (the leak, if any, is open-science diffusion + personnel mobility by EU design, not theft); no individual is accused of wrongdoing. The general academic-knowledge-transfer risk is recorded as a category; the class-generalization to 'Chinese students' is rejected. Overlay edges connect the supply-response cohort into the existing minerals web; the proof core is untouched.
